segments.text: Lesson 37.

translations.zh-Hans: 第37课。

segments.text: The Westhaven Express.

translations.zh-Hans: 开往韦斯特海温的快车。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What was the mistake the author made?

translations.zh-Hans: 作者犯了什么错误？

segments.text: We have learnt to expect that trains will be punctual.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们已经习惯于认为火车会准点。

segments.text: After years of conditioning, most of us have developed an unshakable faith in railway timetables.

translations.zh-Hans: 经过多年的习惯养成，我们大多数人都对铁路时刻表产生了不可动摇的信任。

segments.text: Ships may be delayed by storms, may be cancelled because of bad weather, but trains must be on time.

translations.zh-Hans: 轮船可能因暴风雨而误点，航班可能因恶劣天气而取消，但火车必须准时。

segments.text: Only an exceptionally heavy snowfall might temporarily dislocate railway services.

translations.zh-Hans: 只有特别大的降雪才可能暂时打乱铁路运输。

segments.text: It is all too easy to blame the railway authorities when something does go wrong.

translations.zh-Hans: 一旦出了差错，人们很容易把责任推给铁路部门。

segments.text: The truth is that when mistakes occur, they are more likely to be hours than theirs.

translations.zh-Hans: 事实是，出错时更可能是我们的错，而不是他们的错。

segments.text: After consulting my railway timetable, I noted with satisfaction that there was an express train to Westhaven.

translations.zh-Hans: 查过列车时刻表后，我满意地发现有一趟开往韦斯特海温的快车。

segments.text: It went direct from my local station, and the journey lasted a mere hour and seventeen minutes.

translations.zh-Hans: 它从我当地的车站直达目的地，全程只需1小时17分钟。

segments.text: When I boarded the train, I could not help noticing that a great many local people got on as well.

translations.zh-Hans: 上车时，我不禁注意到许多当地人也上了车。

segments.text: At the time, this did not strike me as odd.

translations.zh-Hans: 当时我并不觉得这有什么奇怪。

segments.text: I reflected that there must be a great many people besides myself who wished to take advantage of this excellent service.

translations.zh-Hans: 我想，除我之外，一定还有很多人想利用这项优质服务。

segments.text: Neither was I surprised when the train stopped at widely, a tiny station a few miles along the line.

translations.zh-Hans: 火车在沿线几英里外的一个小站怀德利停下时，我也没有感到惊讶。

segments.text: Even a mighty express train can be held up by signals.

translations.zh-Hans: 即使是威风凛凛的快车，也可能被信号拦住。

segments.text: But when the train doddled at station after station, I began to wonder.

translations.zh-Hans: 但当列车在一站又一站慢吞吞地停靠时，我开始纳闷了。

segments.text: It suddenly dawned on me that this express was not roaring down the line at ninety miles an hour, but barely chugging along at thirty.

translations.zh-Hans: 我突然明白，这趟“快车”并没有以每小时90英里的速度飞驰，而只是以30英里的速度勉强向前。

segments.text: One hour and seventeen minutes passed, and we had not even covered half the distance.

translations.zh-Hans: 1小时17分钟过去了，我们甚至还没走完一半路程。

segments.text: I asked a passenger if this was the Westhaven Express, but he had not even heard of it.

translations.zh-Hans: 我问一名乘客这是不是韦斯特海温快车，可他连这趟车都没听说过。

segments.text: I determined to lodge a complaint as soon as we arrived.

translations.zh-Hans: 我决定一到站就投诉。

segments.text: Two hours later, I was talking angrily to the station master at Westhaven.

translations.zh-Hans: 两小时后，我正在韦斯特海温愤怒地同站长交涉。

segments.text: When he denied the train's existence, I borrowed his copy of the timetable.

translations.zh-Hans: 他否认有这趟列车，我便借来他的时刻表。

segments.text: There was a note of triumph in my voice when I told him that it was there in black and white.

translations.zh-Hans: 当我告诉他时刻表上白纸黑字写着时，语气中带着胜利的意味。

segments.text: Glancing at it briefly, he told me to look again.

translations.zh-Hans: 他匆匆看了一眼，让我再仔细看看。

segments.text: A tiny asterisk conducted me to a footnote at the bottom of the page.

translations.zh-Hans: 一个小小的星号把我引到了页面底部的一条脚注。

segments.text: It said, This service has been suspended.

translations.zh-Hans: 上面写着：“本次列车已经停运。”
